---
title: "Research"
---

## Overview

My research program centers on developing and applying rigorous statistical methods to questions in political economy and public policy. I focus particularly on:

- **Causal Inference** in observational settings
- **Panel and Time Series** cross-sectional methods
- **Political Economy** of institutions and policy
- **Statistical Computing** in R and Python

---

## Current Projects

### Causal Inference in Observed Populations

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## With Tim Johnson

We are developing new methods for identifying causal effects in settings where randomization is not possible or ethical. Our approach combines design-based and model-based inference strategies.

**Status**: Under review  
**Code**: [GitHub Repository](https://github.com)
:::

---

### Statistical Backwards Induction

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## With Curt Signorino and Muhammet Bas

This project develops statistical methods for analyzing strategic interaction in political science. We extend backwards induction to settings with incomplete information and bounded rationality.

**Status**: Published in *Political Analysis*  
**Award**: Warren Miller Prize  
**Materials**: [Replication archive](https://github.com)
:::

---

## Research Areas

### [Causal Inference](research/causal-inference.qmd)
Methods for identifying and estimating causal effects from observational data, with applications to policy evaluation.

### [Panel Data Methods](research/panel-data.qmd)
Time series cross-sectional models, fixed effects, dynamic panels, and heterogeneous treatment effects.

### [Political Economy](research/political-economy.qmd)
Institutional analysis, policy feedback, and the political economy of redistribution.

---

## Data & Code

All research materials, including data and replication code, are available on my [GitHub](https://github.com/robertwwalker).

I am committed to computational reproducibility and open science. Most projects include:

- Documented R/Python code
- Data (when not restricted)
- Quarto/RMarkdown analysis notebooks
- Instructions for replication

---

## Software

### R Packages

**`panelr`** - Tools for panel data analysis in R  
[CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org) | [GitHub](https://github.com)

**`tscsTools`** - Time series cross-sectional analysis utilities  
[GitHub](https://github.com)

### Teaching Materials

Course materials and lecture slides are available in the [Teaching](../teaching/index.qmd) section.
